ogTitle: Slaty Creek vs Smiths Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Slaty Creek and Smiths Beach.
heading: Slaty Creek vs Smiths Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Slaty Creek, Victoria and Smiths Beach, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Slaty Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Smiths Beach (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Slaty Creek or Smiths Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Slaty Creek has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Smiths Beach scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Slaty Creek scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Smiths Beach scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Slaty Creek or Smiths Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Slaty Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
